page: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core, and runs at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a large 480 MB L3 cache alongside 2 MB L2 and 48 KB L1d caches. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-threaded performance, with Geekbench single-core scoring 2160.0 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 34.22 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% of tested servers. Text processing performance is particularly notable, achieving a top-tier score of 2933.0 in Geekbench. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally align with average performance tiers, including a multi-core Geekbench score of 13138.0 and a multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 17287.9. For lightweight machine learning workloads, the instance demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds of 378.91 tokens/sec using a small 135M LLM, though performance scales down to an average 14.52 tokens/sec for medium 7B models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8i-flex.4xlarge offers a balanced entry point into Intel's eighth-generation compute-optimized hardware class, providing high-speed DDR5 memory and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX without the premium of bundled local storage. The lack of local NVMe storage means storage-heavy databases will require network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is highly suited for compute-bound tasks such as software compilation, single-threaded data compression, text processing pipelines, and lightweight LLM inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C x86_64 processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed. It is configured with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with top 25% performance in single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core tests, alongside top-tier text processing and small-model LLM text generation. Lacking local storage, it relies on network-attached storage. This instance is well-suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, text processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong single-core and text processing performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.4xlarge is an Intel-powered, compute-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and strong single-threaded performance for text processing and compilation workloads.

32.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory with 2.0 GB per core
Strong single-core performance in compression and text processing benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for software compilation, text processing, and lightweight LLM inference
